requirements.source_text: Passwords must be created to ensure they are difficult to guess or compromise.
requirements.applicability: all users who create passwords for organizational systems and applications that require password authentication

requirements.source_text: All passwords must be unique to each system and must not be reused across different services.
requirements.applicability: all users with passwords for organizational systems

requirements.source_text: Passwords must remain confidential and must not be shared with any other individual, including IT personnel.
Passwords must not be stored in plain text (digitally or physically) unless protected by approved secure storage mechanisms such as password managers.
requirements.applicability: systems or storage mechanisms that handle passwords at rest within scope

requirements.source_text: Passwords must have a minimum length of twelve characters.
requirements.applicability: standard user accounts using password authentication

requirements.source_text: Passwords must include a combination of uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters.
requirements.applicability: standard user accounts using password authentication

requirements.source_text: Where systems allow, passphrases should be at least sixteen characters and composed of multiple unrelated words combined with numbers or special characters.
requirements.applicability: systems that allow passphrases for password authentication

requirements.source_text: Users must not reuse previous passwords; the organization will enforce controls to prevent reuse of at least the last ten passwords.
Standard user accounts must update their passwords at least every ninety days.
requirements.applicability: standard user accounts using password authentication

requirements.source_text: Privileged accounts must be updated every sixty days.
Passwords must be changed immediately if there is any suspicion that they have been compromised.
requirements.applicability: all accounts where a password compromise is suspected

requirements.source_text: Multi-factor authentication is required for access to sensitive systems, remote access services, and all privileged accounts.
requirements.applicability: access to sensitive systems, remote access services, and all privileged accounts

requirements.source_text: Accounts will be automatically locked after five failed login attempts.
requirements.applicability: accounts subject to password authentication and login attempt tracking

requirements.source_text: After lockout, the account will be locked for a minimum of fifteen minutes.
requirements.applicability: accounts that are locked due to failed login attempts

requirements.source_text: User sessions must be automatically terminated after fifteen minutes of inactivity.
requirements.applicability: user sessions on systems that support inactivity-based session termination

requirements.source_text: Passwords must not be stored in plain text; they must be protected using strong cryptographic hashing algorithms such as bcrypt or Argon2.
requirements.applicability: password storage mechanisms for organizational authentication systems

requirements.source_text: Passwords must only be transmitted over secure channels that provide encryption.
requirements.applicability: authentication flows that transmit password data between client and server

requirements.source_text: Privileged accounts must use stronger passwords with a minimum length of sixteen characters.
Shared use of privileged accounts is not permitted.
